request.messages: [redacted: candidate evidence omitted from raw judge artifact]

message.reasoning_content:
We are asked to validate extracted ASHRAE Guideline 36 items. We need to accept if the item is "useful, grounded ASHRAE Guideline 36 operational knowledge." Reject if it's legal/front matter, TOC text, generic definitions without operational use, duplicated/noisy extraction, or summaries not supported by evidence. The allowed category_if_not values: null, front_matter, toc, generic_definition, unsupported, duplicate, other. We must return one verdict per candidate_id. The evidence_quote should support the summary. We need to check each candidate.

Let's go through them one by one.

1. cand_d4471498508fb08b: "Airflow Sensor Configuration Selection", section 5.11.5, summary: "Select control logic based on whether airflow sensors are at both inlets or a single discharge sensor." Evidence: "The engineer must select between ventilation logic options: If there are airflow sensors at both inlets to the box , use Section 5.11.5.1 and delete Section 5.11.5.2 . If there is a single airflow sensor at the box discharge, use Section 5.11.5.2 and delete Section 5.11.5.1." This is valid operational guidance. It's about configuration. It is grounded in the evidence. So should be valid.

2. cand_804ba8ced64b740c: "Cooling Mode Damper Control with Dual Inlet Airflow Sensors", section 5.11.5.1, summary mentions cooling-loop resets cold duct airflow setpoint, cold damper modulates, hot damper closed, and if cold-deck SA temp > room temp, limit to minimum. Evidence given: "5.1.15.1. Parallel equipment shall be lead/lag or lead/standby rotated to maintain even wear." The evidence does not match the summary at all! The evidence is about lead/lag rotation, not about cooling mode damper control. So the summary is not supported by the evidence quote. This is a clear case of unsupported or misalignment. So reject as useless or unsupported. Category_if_not: "unsupported".

3. cand_64a53ff0c8f2edd9: "Deadband Mode Damper Control with Dual Inlet Airflow Sensors", section 5.11.5.1, summary: "In deadband state, airflow setpoints are held at last values before deadband; snap-action switch between ducts." Evidence: "When the Zone State is deadband , the active cold duct and hot duct airflow setpoints shall be their last setpoints just before entering deadband. ... This results in a snap-action switch in the damper setpoint ..." This is well-supported. Valid.

4. cand_001e939002b7e137: "Heating Mode Damper Control with Dual Inlet Airflow Sensors", section 5.11.5.1, summary: "In heating state, heating-loop resets hot duct airflow setpoint from minimum to heating maximum; hot duct damper modulates, cold duct damper closed. If hot-deck SA temp < room temp, limit hot duct setpoint to minimum." Evidence: "When the Zone State is heating, the heating-loop output shall reset the active hot duct airflow setpoint from the minimum endpoint to heating maximum endpoint. The hot duct damper shall be modulated ... The cold duct damper shall be closed. 1. If hot-deck supply air temperature from the air handler is less than room temperature, the active hot duct airflow setpoint shall be no higher than the minimum endpoint." The evidence supports it. Valid.

5. cand_01f83708c7b2401f: "Cooling Mode Damper Control with Single Discharge Airflow Sensor", section 5.11.5.2, summary: "In cooling state, cooling-loop resets discharge airflow setpoint from minimum to cooling maximum; cold duct damper modulates to maintain discharge airflow, hot duct damper closed." Evidence: "When the Zone State s is cooling, the cooling-loop output shall reset the active discharge airflow setpoint from the minimum endpoint to cooling maximum endpoint. The cold duct damper shall be modulated ... The hot duct damper shall be closed." Minor typo in evidence ("Zone State s") but it's fine. Supported. Valid.

6. cand_3a32c295bdd4ed31: "Deadband Mode Damper Control with Single Discharge Airflow Sensor", section 5.11.5.2, summary: "In deadband state, discharge airflow setpoint is minimum endpoint, maintained by the damper that was operative before deadband; other damper closed. Snap-action switch." Evidence: "5.1.15.2. Two runtime points shall be defined for each equipment:" Again, evidence is completely unrelated. It's about runtime points, not deadband mode. So unsupported. Reject.

7. cand_5f0b40c3936716c3: "Heating Mode Damper Control with Single Discharge Airflow Sensor", section 5.11.5.2, summary: "In heating state, heating-loop resets discharge airflow setpoint from minimum to heating maximum; hot duct damper modulates to maintain discharge airflow, cold duct damper closed." Evidence: "When the Zone State is heating, the heating-loop output shall reset the active discharge airflow setpoint from the minimum endpoint to heating maximum endpoint. The hot duct damper shall be modulated ... The cooling damper shall be closed." Supported. Valid.

8. cand_96843725048f31e4: "Override to Prevent Backflow (Heating AHU Not Proven On)", section 5.11.5.3, summary: "If heating AHU is not proven ON, close the heating damper." Evidence: "a. If heating air handler is not proven ON, the heating damper shall be closed." Supported. Valid.

9. cand_b567d46d363f783e: "Override to Prevent Backflow (Cooling AHU Not Proven On)", section 5.11.5.3, summary: "If cooling AHU is not proven ON, close the cooling damper." Evidence: "b. If cooling air handler is not proven ON, the cooling damper shall be closed." Supported. Valid.

10. cand_e698208d1ea8d2a7: "Low Airflow Alarm (70% threshold)", section 5.11.6.1, summary: "Generate Level 4 alarm if measured airflow < 70% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint > 0." Evidence: "If the measured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint is greater than zero, generate a Level 4 alarm." Supported. Valid.

11. cand_8bd32af7f5028d79: "Low Airflow Alarm (50% threshold)", section 5.11.6.1, summary: "Generate Level 3 alarm if measured airflow < 50% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint > 0." Evidence: "If the measured airflow is less than 50% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint is greater than zero, generate a Level 3 alarm." Supported. Valid.

12. cand_a0601e25b8d92a19: "Suppress Low Airflow Alarms for Zones with Importance-Multiplier = 0", section 5.11.6.1, summary: "Low airflow alarms shall be suppressed if zone Importance-Multiplier is 0 for its static pressure reset T&R loop." Evidence: "If a zone has an Importance -Multiplier of 0 (see Section 5.1.14.2.a.1) for its static pressure reset T&R control loop, low airflow alarms shall be suppressed for that zone." Supported. Valid.

13. cand_819420ae4a489906: "Airflow Sensor Calibration Alarm", section 5.11.6.2, summary: "Generate Level 3 alarm if fan is off and airflow sensor reading exceeds max(10% of max airflow setpoint, 50 cfm) for 30 minutes." Evidence: "If the fan serving the zone is off and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of the maximum airflow setpoint or 50 cfm for 30 minutes, generate a Level 3 alarm." Supported. Valid.

14. cand_94e726f5be379d35: "Leaking Damper Alarm", section 5.11.6.3, summary: "Generate Level 4 alarm if damper is closed (0%) and airflow sensor reading exceeds max(10% cooling max setpoint, 50 cfm) for 10 minutes while fan is proven ON." Evidence: "If the damper position is 0 %, and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint or 50 cfm for 10 minutes while the fan serving the damper is proven ON, generate a Level 4 alarm." Supported. Valid.

15. cand_54dc0f23a5fe9167: "Custom Thresholds Based on Sensor Accuracy", section 5.11.6.3, summary: "The constant value thresholds for airflow sensor calibration and leaking damper alarms should be set based on the minimum accuracy of the transducer and A/D converter." Evidence: "The constant value thresholds for the airflow sensor calibration and leaking damper alarms are a function of the transducer and A/D converter used to measure air flow. The value used should be determined as the minimum accuracy of the transducer and A/D converter combination." Supported. Valid application guidance.

16. cand_b5f7be7cbf5cb956: "Testing/Commissioning Overrides", section 5.11.7, summary: "Provide software switches to force zone airflow setpoints, damper positions, and reset request-hours accumulators for testing/commissioning." Evidence: "Provide software switches that interlock to a system level point to a. force zone airflow setpoint to zero, b. force zone airflow setpoint to Vcool-max, c. force zone airflow setpoint to Vmin, d. force zone airflow setpoint to Vheat-max, e. force cooling damper full closed/open, f. force heating damper full closed/open, and g. reset request-hours accumulator point to zero (provide one point for each reset type listed in the next section)." Supported. Valid.

17. cand_bdd0cb222db8530c: "Cooling SAT Reset Requests", section 5.11.8.1, summary: "Generate 0–3 requests based on zone temperature above cooling setpoint or Cooling Loop percentage, with suppression after setpoint changes." Evidence: "5.1.18.1. Knowledge of damper and valve position are required for proper generation of T&R reset requests." The evidence is not about cooling SAT reset requests; it's about damper and valve position knowledge. So the summary is not supported. Reject as unsupported.

18. cand_7f72acb00b812be7: "Cold-Duct Static Pressure Reset Requests", section 5.11.8.2, summary: "Generate 0–3 requests based on measured airflow deficit at high damper position, with damper position thresholds." Evidence: "5.11.8.2. Cold-Duct Static Pressure Reset Requests a. If the measured airflow is less than 50% of setpoint while setpoint is greater than zero and the damper position is greater than 95% for 1 minute, send 3 requests. b. Else if the measured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint while setpoint is greater than zero and the damper position is greater than 95% for 1 minute, send 2 requests. c. Else if the damper position is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the damper position is less than 85%. d. Else if the damper position is less than 95%, send 0 requests." This is fully supported. Valid.

So, the invalid ones are: cand_804ba8ced64b740c (evidence mismatch), cand_3a32c295bdd4ed31 (evidence mismatch), cand_bdd0cb222db8530c (evidence mismatch). All others are valid.

Now output JSON.
